---
title: "Provider Course"
format: html
---

## Overview

The **POCUS-CARE Provider Course** is designed to teach the core knowledge and practical skills required to safely perform point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) in emergency and acute care settings.

The programme combines self-directed learning using the open-access [**POCUS-CARE textbook**](../textbook/) with instructor-led lectures, supervised hands-on practice, and competency-based assessment.

## Course objectives

At the end of the course, participants will be able to:

-   Perform focused ultrasound examinations using a systematic scanning approach.
-   Acquire standard cardiac, lung, abdominal and vascular ultrasound views.
-   Recognise common normal and pathological ultrasound findings.
-   Integrate ultrasound findings into clinical decision-making.
-   Perform selected ultrasound-guided procedures.
-   Recognise the limitations of point-of-care ultrasound.

## Target audience

The course is intended for healthcare professionals authorised to perform point-of-care ultrasound within their country of practice.

No previous ultrasound experience is required.

## Prerequisites

Participants should:

-   Be licensed healthcare professionals (according to national regulations).
-   Work in emergency, critical care, primary care, anaesthesia, surgery, or another clinical discipline where POCUS is relevant.

## Course format

The course is implemented in three phases:

-   phase 1: 2-day in-person training session combining interactive lectures with hands-on practical workshops
-   phase 2: Clinical mentorship in the learners' hospital departments 
-   phase 3: Wrap-up and assessment

A refresher session is conducted 6 to 12 weeks, discussing cases encountered during the first months of practice.

## Assessment

Certification of program completion requires:

-   Completion of the pre-test
-   Full attendance to all phases
-   Completion of at least 10 supervised ultrasound exams during the mentorship phase, demonstrated in the provided logbook
-   Successful completion of the post-training written exam
-   A practical skills assessment, demonstrating ability to perform an E-FAST exam within 5 minutes

Participants who successfully complete all assessments receive the **POCUS-CARE Provider Certificate**.

The certificate confirms successful completion of the educational programme. It does **not** constitute a licence or legal authorisation to independently perform ultrasound examinations.

## Upcoming sessions

Visit the [**Course Overview page**](training.qmd) for announcements about future sessions.
